tool_args.label: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments YTD September 2024

payload.scope: Metro-North Railroad, before non-cash liability adjustments

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,395.7 million were $14.2 million or 1.0% favorable to the Forecast.
At the end of September, the total headcount was 6,667
Commutation ridership of 18.6 million was 10.1% above 2023 and 2.0% below the Forecast.
Non-commutation ridership of 30.7 million was 14.4% above 2023 and 6.7% above the Forecast.

tool_args.label: Non-reimbursable operating results unfavorable to Forecast

evidence_refs.source_quote: September YTD non-reimbursable operating results were unfavorable to the Forecast by $32.5 million or 3.4%.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio YTD September 2024

evidence_refs.source_quote: The year-to-date September Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 43.9% which was higher than the Forecast due to higher farebox revenue.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Cost per Passenger YTD September 2024

evidence_refs.source_quote: The year-to-date September Adjusted Cost per Passenger was $23.39 which was lower than the Forecast.
The year-to-date September Revenue per Passenger was $9.27 which was lower than the Forecast.
Labor Expenses: $26.5 million unfavorable to the Forecast.
Non-Labor Expenses: $16.9 million unfavorable to the Forecast.
Payroll was $12.1 million unfavorable to the Forecast
Overtime was $3.7 million unfavorable to the Forecast primarily due to higher programmatic maintenance.
Claims were $5.0 million unfavorable to the Forecast due to an increase in the passenger claim provision for the Valhalla incident.

tool_args.label: Professional Service Contracts unfavorable to Forecast

evidence_refs.source_quote: Professional Service Contracts were $4.3 million unfavorable to the Forecast due to a catch-up for prior years New Haven Line MTA IT allocation costs.

payload.claim_text: Total revenue of $669.3 million was $36.4 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast due to lower capital reimbursements partially offset by higher non-commutation ridership, insurance recoveries for Tropical Storm Ida, higher interest, advertising, and station revenues.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total revenue of $669.3 million was $36.4 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast. This unfavorable variance was due to by lower capital reimbursements partially offset by higher non-commutation ridership, insurance recoveries for Tropical Storm Ida, higher interest, advertising, and station revenues.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

payload.claim_text: Non-reimbursable revenues through September were $21.1 million favorable to the Forecast due to higher ridership, insurance recoveries for Tropical Storm Ida combined with higher interest, advertising, and station revenues partially offset by lower Grand Central Terminal (GCT) net retail revenues.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Non-reimbursable revenues through September were $21.1 million favorable to the Forecast due to higher ridership, insurance recoveries for Tropical Storm Ida combined with higher interest, advertising, and station revenues
